description: Custom traditional Indonesian/Nusantara food dataset for leftover matching.
disclaimer: This dataset is for recipe recommendation and food-waste reduction. It is not medical advice. Users must inspect food carefully and discard unsafe leftovers.

source_policy.summary: Each recipe includes sources. Recipe instructions are paraphrased. Safety notes are conservative and should be verified with food safety authorities.

recipes.name: Nasi Goreng Kampung
recipes.why_leftover_friendly: Nasi goreng is widely described as a way to transform cooked rice, and it accepts small leftover lauk or vegetables.
steps: Check that the rice and any leftover lauk are safe to reuse.
Heat oil and sauté shallot, garlic, chili, or spice paste until fragrant.
Add leftover protein or vegetables and cook until hot.
Add rice, break up clumps, and stir-fry over medium-high heat.
Season with salt, kecap manis, sambal, or the listed spice base.
Cook until the rice is steaming hot, then serve immediately.
safety_notes: Inspect all leftovers first; discard anything sour-smelling, slimy, moldy, unusually discolored, or stored unrefrigerated too long.
Do not use cooked rice left at room temperature for more than 2 hours.
Use rice that was cooled quickly and refrigerated; reheat until steaming hot throughout.
Use meat leftovers only if refrigerated; reheat until the center is hot.
Cook egg until fully set unless the recipe is immediately served and food-safety guidance allows otherwise.
storage_notes: Best eaten immediately after cooking.
Refrigerate any newly cooked leftovers within 2 hours and reheat only once where possible.
recipes.source_notes: At least one source explicitly frames the dish or variant as using leftover food.
